defs: The act of expanding or spreading out; the condition of being expanded; dilation; enlargement.
That which is expanded; expanse; extend surface; as, the expansion of a sheet or of a lake; the expansion was formed of metal.
quotes: The starred expansion of the skies.

defs: Space through which anything is expanded; also, pure space.
quotes: Lost in expansion, void and infinite.

defs: an increase in the production of goods and services over time, and in the volume of business transactions, generally associated with an increase in employment and an increase in the money supply. Opposite of contraction.
The developed result of an indicated operation; as, the expansion of (a + b)2 is a2 + 2ab + b2.
The operation of steam in a cylinder after its communication with the boiler has been cut off, by which it continues to exert pressure upon the moving piston.
The enlargement of the ship mathematically from a model or drawing to the full or building size, in the process of construction.
notes: ☞ Expansion is also used adjectively, as in expansion joint, expansion gear, etc.

defs: an enlarged or extended version of something, such as a writing or discourse; as, the journal article is an expansion of the lecture she gave.
